templateMetadata.description: Calculate campaign return on investment by comparing influenced revenue to campaign costs
templateMetadata.useCase: Measure campaign effectiveness, optimize budget allocation, identify high-ROI channels
customizationPoints: Campaign cost field (ActualCost vs custom field)
prerequisites: Opportunities linked to campaigns via Campaign Influence or Primary Campaign Source

reportMetadata.name: Campaign ROI Analysis - {Period}
reportMetadata.description: Campaign performance ranked by ROI and influenced revenue

reportFilters.customizationNote: Filter to campaigns started in target period
Set to 0 for inactive, or remove to show all
Only include campaigns with cost data

groupingsDown.customizationNote: Can group by Campaign Name for individual campaign analysis

customSummaryFormulas.name: Cost_Per_Response
customSummaryFormulas.description: Average cost to generate one response

customSummaryFormulas.name: Cost_Per_Won_Opportunity
customSummaryFormulas.description: Average cost to generate one won opportunity

chart.note: Bar chart showing ROI % by campaign type, sorted highest to lowest

alternativeReportTypes.when: Using Campaign Influence for multi-touch attribution
alternativeReportTypes.benefits: More accurate revenue attribution across multiple touchpoints

steps: 1. Verify Campaign Influence is enabled (Setup → Marketing → Campaign Influence)
2. Ensure ActualCost field is populated on Campaign object
3. Verify opportunities are linked to campaigns (Campaign Influence or Primary Campaign Source)
4. Update date range filter to target period
5. Deploy report to Marketing Reports folder
6. Create custom summary formulas for ROI, Cost Per Response, etc.

troubleshooting.solution: Check Campaign Influence setup, verify opportunities are linked to campaigns
Populate ActualCost field on Campaign object

troubleshooting.issue: ROI looks unrealistic (too high or too low)
troubleshooting.solution: Verify attribution model (first-touch, last-touch, or multi-touch)
